question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user supply lowerBound=0 and upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE via the registerednode.id query parameter to enumerate the entire registered_node table in a single request, causing the mirror node to expose the full historical ordering of all node registrations and deletions to an unauthenticated caller?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a registerednode.id=gt:0 range parameter that resolves lowerBound to 1 and upperBound to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the query to return nodes in an order that misrepresents the true chronological sequence of node creation events stored in timestamp_range?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the native SQL condition 'deleted is false' by supplying a registerednode.id range that spans IDs of previously deleted and re-created nodes, causing the API to present a reorganized view of node history that omits the deletion events visible in registered_node_history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user pass type=GENERAL_SERVICE to exploit the default fallback mapping in RegisteredNodeMapper.mapRegisteredNodeType() where unknown Short values map to GENERAL_SERVICE, causing nodes of unrecognized types to appear in results and distort the visible history of node type assignments?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user supply order=desc combined with a lowerBound/upperBound range to cause the paginated result set to present registered nodes in reverse chronological order of their registered_node_id, making it appear that newer nodes were registered before older ones and reorganizing the perceived transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Nullable Short type parameter in the native query condition '(:type is null or type @> array[:type]::smallint[])' by passing a type value of 0 or a boundary smallint value, causing the PostgreSQL array containment operator to match nodes it should not, thereby reorganizing which nodes appear in the history view?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user combine registerednode.id=gte:X and registerednode.id=lte:X with the same value X to simulate an equality lookup that bypasses the EQ operator validation in resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds(), causing the history view to show a single node's state without the audit trail that the eq operator path would normally enforce?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the native query only queries registered_node (current state) and never registered_node_history, by iterating through paginated results with manipulated lowerBound/upperBound values to reconstruct a false picture of node history that omits intermediate update states stored only in registered_node_history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user supply limit=MAX_LIMIT with lowerBound=0 and upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE to cause a single query to return the maximum allowed number of registered nodes, and by comparing successive paginated responses detect when nodes have been deleted or re-ordered between requests, effectively observing history reorganization events in real time?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Pageable parameter passed to the native query by manipulating the sort direction to DESC on registered_node_id, causing the query to return results in an order inconsistent with the timestamp_range ordering stored in the database, thereby presenting a reorganized view of when nodes became active?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gap between registered_node_id ordering and timestamp_range ordering by querying with order=asc on registered_node_id while nodes were created out of ID order relative to their consensus timestamps, causing the API to present a history that appears chronologically reorganized compared to the actual on-chain transaction sequence?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user send two simultaneous requests with overlapping lowerBound/upperBound ranges and different type filters to cause the server to return inconsistent snapshots of the registered_node table, making it appear that the same node existed with different types at the same point in history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a timestamp-based filter in the native query to retrieve the current state of registered_node without any reference to the timestamp_range column, causing the API to present a snapshot that cannot be correlated to a specific point in transaction history and thus appears to reorganize the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 'deleted is false' filter in the native query to cause the API to silently omit nodes that were deleted and re-created under the same registered_node_id, making the history appear as a single continuous registration when in fact a deletion event occurred between two creation events?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user supply registerednode.id=lt:1 to force lowerBound=0 and upperBound=0, causing the query to return only nodes with registered_node_id=0 (if any exist), and by observing whether results are returned or not, infer the existence of a node with ID 0 that may not be publicly documented, thereby gaining knowledge to reorganize their understanding of the node history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Pageable sort is applied to registered_node_id but the native query has no ORDER BY clause of its own, relying entirely on Spring Data JPA to append the sort, to determine whether the database could return results in an undefined order if the JPA sort injection fails, causing history to appear reorganized?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the type @> array[:type]::smallint[] PostgreSQL array containment operator by passing a type value that corresponds to a multi-type node (one whose type array contains multiple entries), causing the query to return nodes that match any of their multiple types and thus appear in multiple type-filtered history views simultaneously, reorganizing the perceived type history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Size(max = 2) constraint on registeredNodeIds in RegisteredNodesRequest by supplying exactly two range parameters with operators gt and lt that create a range excluding all existing nodes, causing the API to return an empty result set that misrepresents the actual state of the registered node history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds() logic where two lte operators cause upperBound = Math.min(upperBound, range.getInclusiveValue()) to be applied twice, causing the effective upper bound to be more restrictive than intended and silently omitting nodes from the history view?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds() logic where two gte operators cause lowerBound = Math.max(lowerBound, range.getInclusiveValue()) to be applied twice, causing the effective lower bound to be more restrictive than intended and silently omitting lower-ID nodes from the history view, making it appear those nodes never existed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of registerednode.id=gt:X and registerednode.id=lt:X+1 (where X+1 is a non-integer boundary) to cause resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds() to compute a range that includes or excludes node X depending on how getInclusiveValue() handles the GT operator conversion, causing history to appear reorganized at boundary values?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the native query's lack of a LIMIT clause in the SQL itself (relying on Pageable for limiting) to cause a database-level full table scan of registered_node that, under high concurrency, returns a snapshot inconsistent with the current transaction history due to read committed isolation, making the history appear reorganized?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the next pagination link is constructed as 'registerednode.id=gt:lastId' by the linkFactory, and by following this link repeatedly, cause the API to skip nodes whose registered_node_id was inserted between two paginated requests, creating gaps in the history view that make it appear certain nodes were never registered?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination cursor mechanism where the next link uses 'gt:lastId' to cause a node inserted with an ID equal to lastId between two paginated requests to be permanently skipped in the paginated traversal, effectively reorganizing the history by omitting that node from any complete enumeration?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a consensus timestamp filter in the native query to retrieve registered_node records whose timestamp_range lower endpoint is in the future (if such records exist due to importer bugs), causing the API to present nodes as currently active that have not yet been confirmed by consensus, reorganizing the perceived transaction history?
